title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Altitude Sickness
intro: Herbal teas for Altitude sickness are a natural remedy that involves the consumption of specific herbal infusions to alleviate symptoms associated with high-altitude environments. These teas work by promoting oxygenation of the body, improving circulation, and reducing inflammation, which are all essential in combating altitude sickness. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat this ailment include Ginkgo Biloba, which improves blood flow and reduces oxidative stress; Rhodiola Rosea, which helps the body adapt to high-altitude conditions and reduces fatigue; Peppermint, which aids digestion and reduces nausea; Ginger, which reduces inflammation and improves circulation; and Echinacea, which boosts the immune system and reduces the severity of altitude sickness symptoms. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Licorice root, Chamomile, and Holy Basil are also used to treat altitude sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body to adapt to high altitudes by increasing red blood cell count and improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. This herbal preparation helps to treat altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and improving cardiovascular function. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus teas, including saponins, polysaccharides, and flavonoids, play a crucial role in mitigating the symptoms of altitude sickness by promoting angiogenesis, reducing blood pressure, and improving blood flow. The benefits of using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat altitude sickness include improved acclimatization, reduced risk of high-altitude pulmonary edema (HAPE), and faster recovery from altitude-induced fatigue and dizziness.
preparation_procedure: Gather Astragalus membranaceus dried roots and a tea infuser or a clean piece of cheesecloth.
Measure 2 tablespoons of Astragalus membranaceus dried roots and place them in the tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Heat 1 cup of water to a boil in a pot.
Pour the boiling water over the Astragalus membranaceus dried roots in the tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as needed to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can be used to treat altitude sickness, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as monitoring blood pressure and adjusting dosage, as excessive consumption can lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate conditions like high blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus tea after waking up at altitude.
Repeat drinking 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus tea every 2 hours.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of Astragalus membranaceus per cup of tea.
Increase dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup of tea if needed.
Continue drinking Astragalus membranaceus tea for 2-3 days at altitude.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness, a condition caused by high elevations that can lead to headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The herbal preparation contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to improve blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve oxygen delivery to tissues, making it easier to adapt to high altitudes. By using Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals can benefit from improved respiratory function, reduced risk of high-altitude pulmonary edema, and a faster recovery from altitude sickness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the infuser or muslin bag.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves for 5-7 minutes, then remove the leaves from the water.
Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, Ginkgo biloba teas for altitude sickness appear to be effective due to the antioxidant properties and ability to enhance peripheral and cerebral circulation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms, but possible side effects include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its vasodilating properties which can exacerbate the condition. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, it's essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and be cautious of interactions with other medications, especially those for blood thinning or blood pressure regulation.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea after waking up daily.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of Ginkgo biloba powder with each meal.
Consume 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea before climbing to high altitudes.
Repeat Ginkgo biloba tea consumption every 2 hours while at high altitudes.
Continue using Ginkgo biloba tea for 3-5 days or until symptoms subside.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents present in ginger tea, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, including nausea and headaches, by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain. The warming and expectorant properties of ginger tea also help to relieve congestion and promote digestion, further aiding in the recovery from altitude sickness. By consuming Zingiber officinale teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and effective remedy to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 teaspoon of dried ginger root powder, 1 cup of boiling water, honey or sugar (optional), and a cup.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried ginger root powder into a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the ginger powder in the cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into another cup if you want to remove the ginger particles.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired. Drink the tea as needed for altitude sickness relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a potential remedy to alleviate altitude sickness symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. To minimize the risk of adverse reactions, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming Zingiber officinale teas on an empty stomach or when experiencing pre-existing digestive issues.
preparation_usage: Start with 1/2 cup of Zingiber officinale tea, 3 times a day
Gradually increase to 1 cup, 3-4 times a day as needed
Take Zingiber officinale tea within 1-2 hours of ascending to high altitude
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage or frequency of usage accordingly
Continue usage for 2-3 days after reaching high altitude, then gradually taper off

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, a condition caused by low oxygen levels at high elevations. The adaptogenic properties of Panax ginseng help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with altitude sickness, while also improving circulation and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides and antioxidants, have been shown to help mitigate the effects of high altitude by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the development of altitude sickness. Regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas can provide benefits such as improved physical performance, faster acclimatization, and reduced risk of altitude-related illnesses, making it a valuable adjunctive treatment for individuals ascending to high elevations.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water, 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Bring water to a boil in a kettle or pot, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Place the dried Panax ginseng root in the tea infuser or strainer, and put it into the pot with the simmering water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer from the pot.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve hot, adding honey or lemon to taste if needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to alleviate altitude sickness symptoms, but its use may cause side effects such as insomnia, headaches, and rapid heart rate in some individuals. To safely use Panax ginseng teas for altitude sickness, it is recommended to start with a low dose, gradually increase as needed, and avoid consuming it with other stimulants or medications, especially those that affect blood pressure or cardiovascular health.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Panax ginseng tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of ginseng per cup of tea.
Gradually increase to 1 teaspoon per cup if needed.
Drink tea 30 minutes before climbing and after exercise.
Continue drinking for 3-5 days or as directed by a doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea. The herbal preparation's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promote the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines plays a crucial role in treating this ailment. Bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, individuals can benefit from reduced severity and frequency of altitude sickness symptoms, making it a valuable natural remedy for high-altitude travel.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the cooled water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used as an alternative treatment for altitude sickness, but it may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, irregular heart rhythms, and electrolyte imbalances due to its licorice root content, which can disrupt hormone regulation. To minimize these risks, it is essential to consume Glycyrrhiza glabra teas in moderation and for a limited period, as prolonged use can lead to hormonal imbalances and other complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day
Take 1-2 teaspoons of Glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 1 cup of water
Start with low dose (250mg) and increase as needed up to 500mg
Drink tea or mix powder with water 1 hour before ascent and after
Monitor body response and adjust dosage or usage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness, a condition characterized by nausea, headaches, and fatigue at high elevations. The adaptogenic properties of this herbal preparation help to mitigate the effects of high-altitude stress by regulating the body's response to changes in oxygen levels. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis, including schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides, help to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote antioxidant activity, which contributes to the alleviation of altitude sickness symptoms. The benefits of using Schisandra chinensis teas to treat altitude sickness include improved physical performance, reduced risk of altitude-related illnesses, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of Schisandra chinensis dried berries.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and add the Schisandra chinensis dried berries to the pot.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 1 hour before ascending to high altitude.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can be used to potentially alleviate altitude sickness symptoms, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting, as well as increased urination and heart rate. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including monitoring blood pressure and avoiding use in individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions or pregnancy.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Schisandra chinensis tea daily for altitude sickness relief
Start drinking tea 2-3 days before ascending to high altitude
Take 2-3 teaspoons of Schisandra chinensis powder per cup of boiling water
Drink tea 2-3 times a day, or as needed for altitude sickness symptoms
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage and treatment plan

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas, made from the bark of the Cinchona tree, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness, also known as acute mountain sickness (AMS). The antimalarial properties of quinine, a bioactive constituent found in Cinchona officinalis, help to alleviate the symptoms of AMS by reducing inflammation and improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. The quinoline alkaloids present in this herbal preparation, such as quinine and quinidine, help to counteract the effects of high altitude on the body by increasing red blood cell production and improving cardiovascular function. By consuming Cinchona officinalis teas, individuals can benefit from reduced symptoms of altitude sickness, including headaches, dizziness, and nausea, allowing them to ascend to higher elevations with greater ease and comfort.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer.
Measure 2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and place it in a strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Cinchona officinalis bark in the strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then carefully lift the strainer and discard the bark.
Drink the tea and consider repeating every 8 hours as needed to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be effective in treating altitude sickness, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as headaches and dizziness. To minimize the risks, take the teas in moderation, monitor your body's reaction, and avoid consuming them if you experience severe or persistent side effects, as well as if you have underlying conditions such as high blood pressure or are taking medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Cinchona officinalis tea 3 times a day
Start 24 hours before ascending to high altitude
Continue drinking for 2-3 days after reaching high altitude
Take 2-3 teaspoons of Cinchona bark powder per cup of tea
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, a condition often experienced by individuals traveling to high elevations. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to mitigate the effects of altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic benefits by modulating the immune system and improving cardiovascular function. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas may help to reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms, such as headaches and fatigue, allowing individuals to acclimate more easily to high-altitude environments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes before ascending to high altitudes.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided content, Echinacea purpurea teas are not specifically mentioned for the treatment of altitude sickness, but the plant has potential for adjuvant symptomatic therapy in respiratory conditions, such as cough, and is one of the medicinal plants that may hold therapeutic value, but no direct information on its application for altitude sickness is available.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be beneficial in treating altitude sickness, but its use may lead to side effects such as allergic reactions, skin rashes, and increased heart rate. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, and to avoid consuming it if you have any underlying health conditions, such as high blood pressure, and to drink plenty of water to prevent dehydration.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been used traditionally to treat altitude sickness, a condition caused by low oxygen levels at high elevations. The sedative and anti-anxiety properties of Valeriana officinalis teas help to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis teas, including valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the nervous system and reducing inflammation. Drinking Valeriana officinalis teas can help to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness, allowing individuals to acclimate to high elevations and reducing the risk of more severe health complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Valeriana officinalis roots, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the Valeriana officinalis roots in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the roots and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the roots.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to American Journal of Health-System Pharmacy: AJHP: official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists, Valeriana officinalis teas have been studied for their potential benefits, including the treatment of insomnia, but there is no specific information on their use for altitude sickness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, but possible side effects include drowsiness, headaches, and stomach upset, particularly when consumed in large amounts or in combination with other medications. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, take them in moderation, start with a low dose, and be aware of your body's sensitivity to its sedative effects, especially if you plan to engage in physical activities at high altitudes.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to its properties that help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea. The herbal preparation's ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation makes it an effective remedy for this ailment. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include carnosic acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and rosmarinic acid, which exhibits vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory activities. By consuming rosemary tea, individuals can benefit from its ability to reduce symptoms of altitude sickness, improve overall well-being, and enhance their ability to adapt to high-altitude environments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and a cup of boiling water.
Place the leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea every 2-3 hours to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used as a herbal remedy for altitude sickness, but its use may result in side effects such as drowsiness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its carminative and vasodilatory properties. To minimize the risk of adverse reactions, it is essential to use Rosmarinus officinalis teas in moderation and avoid consuming it with other medications or stimulants, as this may exacerbate side effects like increased heart rate and blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosa rugosa teas have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular function, thereby enhancing the body's ability to adapt to high altitudes. The bioactive constituents of Rosa rugosa teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, play a crucial role in mitigating the effects of altitude sickness by promoting vasodilation and improving blood flow to the brain and other vital organs. By consuming Rosa rugosa teas, individuals can benefit from reduced risk of altitude sickness, improved physical performance, and faster recovery from high-altitude activities.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosa rugosa flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea every 2 hours as needed for altitude sickness relief.
Repeat the process as needed, or consult a healthcare professional for further guidance.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosa rugosa teas can be used as a natural remedy to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and nausea in some individuals. When using Rosa rugosa teas to treat altitude sickness, it is essential to take precautions, including starting with small doses, monitoring your body's reaction, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications or substances.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax quinquefolius teas, also known as American ginseng, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness, a condition that occurs when the body is unable to adapt to high elevations. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue. The bioactive constituents, including ginsenosides and other compounds, help to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, making it easier for the body to adapt to high altitudes. By consuming Panax quinquefolius teas, individuals can benefit from reduced symptoms of altitude sickness and improved overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Panax quinquefolius roots. You can buy them from a health food store or online.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried roots and place them in a small saucepan.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the saucepan. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax quinquefolius teas can be used to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to the increased sensitivity to the tea's active compounds. When using Panax quinquefolius teas to treat altitude sickness, precautions should be taken to avoid over-consumption, as excessive amounts may lead to gastrointestinal upset and interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis teas, also known as Baikal skullcap tea, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids, such as baicalein and baicalin, present in this herbal preparation help to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting cardiovascular health. By activating the body's natural response to high altitudes, Scutellaria baicalensis teas can help to reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms, including headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The benefits of using Scutellaria baicalensis teas to treat altitude sickness include improved tolerance to high altitudes, reduced risk of altitude-related complications, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Scutellaria baicalensis root.
Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the dried root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the Scutellaria baicalensis tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate altitude sickness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Scutellaria baicalensis teas can be used to treat altitude sickness, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, take Scutellaria baicalensis teas in moderation, and be aware of any allergic reactions or interactions with other medications, especially when using it for an extended period or at high altitudes.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness, a condition characterized by headaches, nausea, and fatigue that occurs when the body is unable to adapt to high elevations. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Curcuma longa teas, particularly the bioactive constituents curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, help to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. By consuming Curcuma longa teas, individuals can reduce the severity of altitude sickness, allowing them to acclimate to high elevations more efficiently and comfortably. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat altitude sickness include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of complications, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Curcuma longa root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the root.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, it does not specifically mention the use of Curcuma longa teas for altitude sickness, however, it does mention that Curcuma longa has been traditionally used for various ailments, including its potential pharmacological properties such as anti-inflammatory, and it is one of the most popular plants used in Mauritius.

preparations.herb_desc: Andrographis paniculata teas have gained attention for their potential to alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness, a condition characterized by headaches, nausea, and fatigue at high elevations. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including andrographolide, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms. The andrographolide in Andrographis paniculata teas is believed to help treat altitude sickness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as headaches and nausea. By consuming Andrographis paniculata teas, individuals may experience benefits including reduced discomfort, improved respiratory function, and enhanced overall well-being during high-altitude travel.
preparation_procedure: Gather Andrographis paniculata dried leaves (about 2 teaspoons) and a cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain into another cup.
Add 1-2 cups of cold water to the strained tea, if desired for a milder taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Andrographis paniculata tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed for altitude sickness relief.
Consider consulting a healthcare professional for proper dosage and guidance before using Andrographis paniculata for altitude sickness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Andrographis paniculata teas can be used to treat altitude sickness, but consuming it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea, especially if taken in large quantities or for an extended period. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach or when experiencing dehydration.

preparations.herb_desc: Ephedra sinica teas have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness, a condition that occurs when the body is unable to adapt to high elevations. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat altitude sickness by increasing oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, improving circulation, and enhancing the body's adaptation to high altitudes. The bioactive constituents of Ephedra sinica teas, including ephedrine and other alkaloids, help to stimulate the body's natural response to altitude sickness by increasing heart rate and blood pressure, which in turn improves oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. The benefits of using Ephedra sinica teas to treat altitude sickness include rapid relief from symptoms such as headaches and fatigue, improved physical performance at high altitudes, and a reduced risk of developing more severe altitude sickness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ephedra sinica leaves. You can find them in most herbal stores or online.
Wash the leaves with clean water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Ephedra sinica powder (you can make your own by drying the leaves) or 2 tablespoons of fresh leaves.
Steep the powder or leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms. Consult a doctor before using Ephedra sinica tea, especially if you have pre-existing medical conditions.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ephedra sinica teas can be used to treat altitude sickness, but they may cause side effects such as headaches, dizziness, and an increased heart rate due to the presence of ephedrine, a stimulant that can exacerbate the condition in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming these teas with other stimulants or caffeine-containing substances.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, derived from the flowers of the English lavender plant, have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness. The herbal preparation's calming and anti-inflammatory properties help to treat this ailment by reducing stress and anxiety, while also relieving headaches and nausea. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, possess sedative and anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to the tea's therapeutic effects. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia teas can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms, promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and improving overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the dried flowers in the infuser or cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness, but its use may lead to side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its potential to exacerbate dehydration and interact with medications. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat altitude sickness, it is recommended to drink it in moderation, stay well-hydrated, and avoid consuming it for extended periods of time to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort tea, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their adaptogenic properties and ability to help the body acclimate to high elevations. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue associated with altitude sickness. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Hypericum perforatum teas may also contribute to their beneficial effects in treating altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Overall, the use of Hypericum perforatum teas to treat altitude sickness is believed to offer benefits such as improved oxygenation, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum leaves, stems, and flowers.
Chop the gathered Hypericum perforatum into small pieces.
Combine 1 cup of chopped Hypericum perforatum with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Hypericum perforatum tea, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Hypericum perforatum teas have antioxidant properties and high phenolic compounds content, similar to other teas known for their high antioxidant potential.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat altitude sickness, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. When using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat altitude sickness, it is essential to be cautious with its dosage, as excessive consumption can lead to increased side effects, and it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Ilex paraguariensis teas, also known as yerba mate, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness, a condition characterized by headaches, nausea, and fatigue at high elevations. The herbal preparation's diuretic properties help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness. The bioactive constituents, including caffeine, theobromine, and theobin, in Ilex paraguariensis teas contribute to its effectiveness in treating this ailment by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and enhancing oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. Regular consumption of Ilex paraguariensis teas has been shown to offer benefits in alleviating altitude sickness symptoms, promoting faster acclimatization to high elevations, and reducing the risk of related complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ilex paraguariensis leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup or mug.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried leaves and place them in the cup or mug.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a strainer or fine mesh, and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea as needed to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms, ideally 2-3 cups per day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ilex paraguariensis teas can be used as a potential treatment for altitude sickness, but its use may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and increased heart rate. To avoid these side effects, it is recommended to consume Ilex paraguariensis teas in moderation, especially when first starting to use it, and to increase hydration levels to minimize the risk of dehydration and other complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Angelica sinensis teas, also known as dong quai, have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. These herbal preparations help to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness by regulating blood pressure, improving blood circulation, and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Angelica sinensis, including ferulic acid and ligustilide, help to scavenge free radicals and mitigate inflammation, thereby reducing the severity of altitude sickness. By consuming Angelica sinensis teas, individuals can benefit from improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced oxygen delivery to tissues, making it an effective herbal remedy for treating altitude sickness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Angelica sinensis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Angelica sinensis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the root.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day.
Consider combining with other altitude sickness remedies and consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Angelica sinensis teas for altitude sickness are not mentioned, but it is mentioned that Angelica sinensis (Danggui) plays a significant role in the treatment of interstitial lung disease (ILD) by inhibiting inflammatory factor release, anti-oxidative injury, and interfering with collagen production, etc., which might be beneficial for treating other respiratory conditions but there is no direct evidence regarding its use for altitude sickness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Angelica sinensis teas can be used to alleviate some symptoms of altitude sickness, but potential side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach discomfort in some individuals. To minimize risks, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and be aware of any allergic reactions or interactions with other medications.

preparation_study: According to Alternative medicine review : a journal of clinical therapeutic, Astragalus membranaceus teas have been studied for their therapeutic benefit in treatment of ischemic heart disease, myocardial infarction, heart failure, and relief of anginal pain, but there is no information provided on its use for altitude sickness.
